req.title: System-Admin creates document
req.statement: An authenticated system-admin shall be able to create a document through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers document creation via UI form for a system-admin. I derive test cases using: EP for the happy-path (valid admin submits all mandatory fields), NEG for missing mandatory field (400 validation error) and unauthenticated access (401), DT for role-forbidden scenario (403), BVA for the document title at its maximum allowed length boundary, and STC for the state transition from draft-to-created including audit record verification. Together these satisfy the minimum positive/negative/boundary coverage mandate while tracing every stated expected outcome and error pathway.

tests.name: test_admin_creates_document_with_all_mandatory_fields
preconditions: No pre-existing document with the test title exists
actions: POST /documents with a fully populated, valid payload as system-admin
expected: Response body contains a non-empty entity id
Audit record is written for the created document
trace: R-WEB-029: response 200 or 201 with entity id
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_admin_creates_document_with_all_mandatory_fields(
    admin_session,        # <fixture> returns authenticated requests.Session for admin
    api_base_url,         # <fixture> returns base URL string
    audit_log_reader,     # <fixture> returns helper with .latest(entity_id) method
    unique_title,         # <fixture> returns a unique string per test run
    cleanup_document,     # <fixture> deletes doc by id on teardown
):
    payload = {
        "title": unique_title,
        "type": "policy",
        "content": "Initial content.",
        "owner": "admin"
    }
    resp = admin_session.post(f"{api_base_url}/documents", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201), f"Unexpected status: {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    entity_id = body.get("id") or body.get("documentId")
    assert entity_id, "Response must include a non-empty entity id"
    cleanup_document(entity_id)
    audit = audit_log_reader.latest(entity_id)
    assert audit is not None, "Audit record must exist for the created document"
    assert audit.get("action") == "CREATE"

tests.name: test_create_document_missing_mandatory_title_returns_400
actions: POST /documents with payload where mandatory 'title' field is omitted
expected: Response body contains a validation error referencing 'title'
trace: R-WEB-029: mandatory fields must be validated
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_create_document_missing_mandatory_title_returns_400(
    admin_session,
    api_base_url,
    audit_log_reader,
):
    payload = {
        "type": "policy",
        "content": "Content without a title.",
        "owner": "admin"
    }
    resp = admin_session.post(f"{api_base_url}/documents", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 400, f"Expected 400, got {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    errors = body.get("errors") or body.get("validationErrors") or []
    field_names = [e.get("field", "").lower() for e in errors]
    assert any("title" in f for f in field_names), (
        f"Validation error must reference 'title'; got: {field_names}"
    )
    # Confirm no audit record was created for a phantom entity
    phantom_id = body.get("id")
    if phantom_id:
        audit = audit_log_reader.latest(phantom_id)
        assert audit is None, "No audit record should exist for a failed creation"

tests.name: test_create_document_unauthenticated_returns_401
actions: POST /documents with a valid payload but without any auth credentials
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_create_document_unauthenticated_returns_401(
    api_base_url,
    unique_title,
):
    payload = {
        "title": unique_title,
        "type": "policy",
        "content": "Some content.",
        "owner": "unknown"
    }
    # Deliberately use a bare session with no credentials
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{api_base_url}/documents",
        json=payload,
        timeout=10
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 401, f"Expected 401, got {resp.status_code}"

tests.name: test_create_document_forbidden_role_returns_403
preconditions: User is authenticated but has role 'viewer' (not admin)
actions: POST /documents with a valid payload using a viewer-role session
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_create_document_forbidden_role_returns_403(
    viewer_session,   # <fixture> returns authenticated requests.Session for viewer role
    api_base_url,
    unique_title,
):
    payload = {
        "title": unique_title,
        "type": "policy",
        "content": "Content from a viewer.",
        "owner": "viewer"
    }
    resp = viewer_session.post(f"{api_base_url}/documents", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 403, f"Expected 403, got {resp.status_code}"

tests.name: test_create_document_title_at_maximum_length_boundary
preconditions: Maximum allowed title length is 255 characters (per field constraint)
actions: POST /documents with title exactly 255 characters long
Capture response status and entity id
Then POST /documents with title of 256 characters
expected: First request (255 chars): HTTP status 200 or 201, entity id present
Second request (256 chars): HTTP status 400, validation error on 'title'
trace: R-WEB-029: mandatory fields must be validated
R-WEB-029: response 200 or 201 with entity id

tests.name: test_create_document_state_transition_draft_to_created_with_audit
preconditions: System is in state: no document with the target title exists (pre-creation state)
actions: POST /documents with a valid payload to trigger the DRAFT -> CREATED transition
Retrieve the created document via GET /documents/{id}
Verify audit log entry reflects the state transition
expected: Pre-state: document absent (404 on GET or empty list)
Audit log action is 'CREATE' with the correct entity id and admin actor
trace: R-WEB-029: response 200 or 201 with entity id
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_create_document_state_transition_draft_to_created_with_audit(
    admin_session,
    api_base_url,
    audit_log_reader,
    unique_title,
    cleanup_document,
):
    # Pre-state: confirm document absent
    search = admin_session.get(
        f"{api_base_url}/documents", params={"title": unique_title}
    )
    items = search.json().get("items", search.json() if isinstance(search.json(), list) else [])
    assert len(items) == 0, "Document must not exist before test"

    # Trigger state transition
    payload = {"title": unique_title, "type": "memo", "content": "Audit test.", "owner": "admin"}
    resp = admin_session.post(f"{api_base_url}/documents", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201)
    entity_id = resp.json().get("id") or resp.json().get("documentId")
    assert entity_id
    cleanup_document(entity_id)

    # Post-state: document exists and is in created/active state
    get_resp = admin_session.get(f"{api_base_url}/documents/{entity_id}")
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200
    status = get_resp.json().get("status", "").lower()
    assert status in ("created", "active"), f"Unexpected document status: {status}"

    # Audit verification
    audit = audit_log_reader.latest(entity_id)
    assert audit is not None
    assert audit.get("action") == "CREATE"
    assert str(entity_id) in str(audit.get("entityId", ""))
    assert "admin" in audit.get("actor", "").lower()
